commands.review_summary:
The PR adds first-class A2UI dataModelUpdate.valueArray parsing, storage depth checks, array redaction, skill docs updates, and parser/store/rendering regression coverage.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main documents valueArray but the WinUI parser has no ValueArray member or parse branch, so a valueArray dataModelUpdate cannot survive into the data model.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor action is needed for real behavior proof, then maintainers can do normal review/undraft handling; there is no narrow repair for automation to perform.

target.title: Make A2UI dataModelUpdate.valueArray first-class
target.body:
## Summary

The v0.8 `dataModelUpdate.valueArray` typed value was **silently dropped** by the native WinUI A2UI parser ‚Äî `DataModelEntry` had no `ValueArray` field and `ToJsonNode()` returned `null` for it. Only `valueString`/`valueNumber`/`valueBoolean`/`valueMap` were handled.

This broke seeding an array into a surface's data model ‚Äî most visibly a multi-select `MultipleChoice` bound to a path could never be pre-populated. The same agent JSONL renders correctly on Mac/main and the Lit reference (which treat `valueArray` as a standard v0.8 value per `docs/a2ui/protocol.md` ¬ß2.2 and `docs/a2ui/grading.md`), so this was a silent cross-platform parity gap.

## What changed

- **Parser** (`A2UIProtocol.cs`): add `DataModelEntry.ValueArray`; `ToJsonNode()` emits a real `JsonArray`; new `ParseValueArray`/`ParseArrayElement` handle value-typed object elements (`{"valueString":"x"}`), bare-primitive tolerance (`["a",1,true]`), nested maps/arrays, and preserve a JSON `null` (or value-less `{}`) as a **stable index slot** so position-sensitive consumers don't see shifted indices.
- **DoS guard** (`DataModelStore.cs`): the existing 32-deep depth bound now recurses `valueArray` as well as `valueMap`, so deep nesting can't be smuggled in through an array.
- **Security** (`SecretRedactor.cs`): the `JsonArray` redaction branch now checks each element path against the registered/denylist set **before** recursing (mirroring the object branch). Previously arrays only recursed, so a secret seeded into an array (e.g. an obscured `TextField` bound to `/codes/0`) could leak through `canvas.a2ui.dump`.
- **Docs** (`SKILL.md`): correct the stale "arrays are not first-class" note.

## Why it's safe

- Purely additive on the wire; existing scalar/`valueMap` behavior is unchanged and regression-tested.
- `System.Text.Json` `TryGetValue<T>` is strict (no cross-kind coercion), so the bare-primitive string‚Üíbool‚Üínumber detection can't mis-tag a value.
- Malformed "both map and array set" deterministically prefers the map; never throws.
- The redaction change can only **over**-redact, never under-redact.

## Tests

- `A2UIDataModelArrayTests.cs` (new) ‚Äî 11 parser tests incl. null-slot and value-less-object-slot cases.
- `SecretRedactorTests.cs` ‚Äî 3 new array-redaction regressions (registered element, registered array parent, denylisted scalar in array).
- `A2UIDataModelStoreTests.cs` (new) ‚Äî store base-path landing + `valueArray` depth-guard rejection + `valueMap` depth regression.
- `A2UIControlMatrixTests.cs` ‚Äî end-to-end: `MultipleChoice` (multi) seeded via `valueArray` asserts both the snapshot array **and** the rendered ListView preselection (parser ‚Üí store ‚Üí renderer round-trip).

## Validation

`./build.ps1` (all 5 projects) ¬∑ `OpenClaw.Shared.Tests` 2065 ¬∑ `OpenClaw.Tray.Tests` 975 ¬∑ A2UI `OpenClaw.Tray.UITests` 63 ‚Äî all passing (`OPENCLAW_REPO_ROOT` set per AGENTS.md). Two independent rubber-duck reviews (gpt-5.5 + claude-opus-4.8) run; the one blocking finding (secret-in-array) was fixed and a final gpt-5.5 pass confirmed no remaining blockers.

## Known limitations (pre-existing, out of scope)

- Nested array/map **breadth** is bounded only by the 1 MiB line cap ‚Äî identical to the pre-existing `valueMap` behavior; a shared element-count budget would be a separate cross-cutting change.
- `BuildActionContext` deep-clones a non-secret subtree without redacting nested secret-named descendants ‚Äî pre-existing, spans `valueMap` too, not introduced here.
target.author: bkudiess
